uuid.claimURLBase: To claim your node, after starting it, go to the app's dashboard and then visit the following link: https://earnapp.com/r/sdk-node-

device_limits.notes: 1 device per IP for meaningful earnings. Multiple devices on same IP are deprioritized. Community-reported 15-device limit unverified in current official docs.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Bright Data IP reputation scoring. Datacenter ASNs filtered. Daily per-device bandwidth throttled (200-400 MB/day reported).

warnings: EULA explicitly prohibits Docker and Linux hosting usage
Third-party unmaintained Docker image (last updated 2023-06)
Usage may violate Bright Data Terms of Service
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Explicitly targets residential IPs for proxy resale via Bright Data network. Datacenter ASNs are frequently blocked.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: System architecture prioritizes unique IPs; multiple nodes on one IP earn negligible rewards. EULA bans Docker/Linux entirely.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Corrected install_limit to 1 to align with IP priority policies. Kept residential layer. Users should be warned about EULA risk.
app_details.confidence_notes: Terms of Use and Bright Data integration verified from official EULA and community research.

device_limits.notes: Decentralized edge cloud platform. No hard limits documented.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Markets itself as 'decentralized edge cloud'. IP acceptance policy unclear.

warnings: Community Docker image only — no official container verified
Requires deeper research before promoting to supported
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Markets itself as 'decentralized edge cloud'. IP acceptance policy unclear. Conservative residential classification.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: No documented device caps. Decentralized platform.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Promoted to supported. Docker image verified on Docker Hub.
app_details.confidence_notes: Limited external validation. Requires deeper research before promoting to supported.

machine_id.claimURLBase: After starting, run 'docker logs <container> 2>&1 | grep "Device Key"' to get your device key. Register it at the Spide dashboard.

device_limits.notes: Requires machine ID to regenerate device keys. Designed for multi-node deployments.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Payout rates vary by IP type. Verified support for residential, Linux, and Docker on multiple IP types.

app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Verified support for residential, Linux, and Docker on multiple IP types. Payout rates vary by IP type.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: Designed for multi-node deployments. Requires machine ID to regenerate device keys.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Promoted to supported. Docker image verified on Docker Hub.
app_details.confidence_notes: Community tests confirm functionality, but it is early-stage.

entries.name: DAWN

app_details.removal_history: Moved to third-party image, unreliable service. Browser-extension product with no official Docker support.
warnings: No official Docker image — relies on third-party browser-container workaround
Third-party image shared with Teneo and Gradient
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Browser-extension product. No official Docker support. Third-party browser-container workaround only.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: No other project supports Dawn as a standalone app. carbon2029/dockweb is the only Docker approach and unreliable. Browser extension not suitable for headless Docker.

entries.additional_details: Moved to third-party image, unreliable service

entries.name: GRADIENT

device_limits.notes: Gradient pivoted significantly toward AI infrastructure (Lattica/Parallax protocols) after raising $10M in June 2025. Sentry Node Open Beta concluded. Bandwidth-sharing product status unclear.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Sentry nodes used for connectivity mapping, Parallax nodes for GPU/RAM inference. Original product was browser-extension based — fundamentally incompatible with headless Docker.

warnings: Company pivoted to AI infrastructure (Parallax) — bandwidth node future uncertain
Third-party Docker image (carbon2029/dockweb) not maintained by Gradient
Same image shared with Dawn and Teneo — reliability concerns
No official Docker image exists
Sentry Node Open Beta has concluded
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: The 'Sentry Node' product is primarily browser-extension based for residential connectivity. Parallax AI pivot moves toward compute, not bandwidth.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: Primarily a liveness mapping tool; multiple instances per IP offer diminishing utility. Conservative 1-instance limit applied due to instability.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Core product is now Parallax AI. Sentry Node is in transition. Third-party browser-wrapper image (carbon2029/dockweb) is unreliable. Consider deprecation.
app_details.confidence_notes: Official AI pivot documented in June 2025. Sentry Node Open Beta concluded. No official Docker image exists.
app_details.removal_history: Removed 2026-05-15 after parent company pivoted to Parallax AI infrastructure. Sentry Node Open Beta concluded; bandwidth-sharing product no longer viable. Third-party image carbon2029/dockweb (shared with Dawn/Teneo) was the only Docker route and is unreliable.

entries.additional_details: AI pivot to Parallax — Sentry Node bandwidth product superseded

app_details.removal_history: Removed due to Docker image unavailability and service instability. No official Docker image exists. Significant VC backing but no official container as of 2026.

device_limits.notes: Browser-extension primary architecture. No official Docker support as of March 2026.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Residential-only by design. VPN and datacenter IPs blocked. Evolved into major DePIN player on Solana.

warnings: No official Docker image — community-maintained only
Worth monitoring — if official Docker image is released, reconsider
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Residential-only by design. VPN and datacenter IPs blocked. Browser-extension primary architecture.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: No official Docker support. Browser extension model limits practical deployment.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: No official Docker image exists. Multiple third-party images exist (XternA websocket vs carbon2029 browser-wrapper). Worth monitoring for official Docker release.
app_details.confidence_notes: Major DePIN player on Solana but no official container as of March 2026. Two different community approaches exist.

entries.name: PEER2PROFIT

manual.instructions: Use any VNC client to connect to localhost:5901 using the password money4band and follow the onscreen instruction to complete the setup

app_details.removal_history: Docker Hub image was pulled (GitHub issue #56). Website has been down. Service fully discontinued.

device_limits.notes: Service discontinued. Website down. Docker Hub image pulled.

app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Service discontinued. Website down. Docker Hub image pulled.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Docker Hub image was pulled. Website down. All three projects use different third-party images (mrcolorrain, xterna, enwaiax). Service is fully dead.
app_details.confidence_notes: YouTube reports and Trustpilot reviews confirm service shutdown. Multiple community images exist but service backend is offline.

entries.additional_details: Docker Hub image pulled (#56) / service discontinued

entries.name: PROXYBASE

device_limits.notes: Service reportedly moved domains. Very sparse documentation. No reliable information found in 2025-2026 research.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Insufficient data to assess IP policy.

warnings: Domain instability — service may have moved or shut down
Zero accessible official documentation
Verify service is alive before deploying
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Insufficient data to assess IP policy. Service domain instability prevents verification.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: Zero accessible official documentation. No verified limits exist.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Domain instability and zero documentation. Verify service is alive before deploying. Consider deprecation.
app_details.confidence_notes: No accessible documentation or active service site found during research.
app_details.removal_history: Removed 2026-05-15 due to domain instability. Service may have shut down or migrated; zero accessible documentation found in 2025-2026 research. No verifiable signal that the peer-program is still paying out.

entries.additional_details: Domain instability — service may have shut down

app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Service fully discontinued. Other projects have either removed or disabled it. mrcolorrain image is the only available Docker image.

entries.name: TENEO

ip_policy.quality_notes: Points-based reward system. Primarily residential browser extension.

Browser-extension model not suitable for headless Docker

app_details.recommendation_reasoning: No other project supports Teneo. carbon2029/dockweb is the only Docker approach and unreliable.

entries.additional_details: Moved to third-party image, unreliable service

app_details.removal_history: Removed by M4B community vote in March 2026 due to excessive CPU/RAM/temperature usage. Unofficial Docker image. Resource pattern suggests compute-sharing rather than bandwidth-sharing.

device_limits.notes: Community vote removed it from M4B due to excessive resource usage.

ip_policy.quality_notes: Resource pattern (excessive CPU/RAM) suggests compute-sharing rather than bandwidth-sharing.

warnings: Excessive CPU/RAM/temperature usage reported by community
No official Docker image — community GHCR image only
Removed by community vote March 2026
app_details.compose_layer_reasoning: Compute-sharing rather than bandwidth-sharing pattern. Excessive CPU/RAM usage reported.
app_details.install_limit_reasoning: Removed by M4B community vote. Resource pattern unsuitable for bandwidth-sharing tool.
app_details.recommendation_reasoning: Removed by M4B community vote March 2026 due to excessive resource usage. Multiple Docker images exist. Some projects throttle CPU to mitigate. Consider keeping as opt-in with resource warnings.
app_details.confidence_notes: Community vote and multiple Docker image variants confirm problematic resource pattern.

device_limits.notes: Limited documentation. Other projects support it.

warnings: Reported 522 errors in community

app_details.removal_history: Removed 2026-05-15 due to persistent HTTP 522 errors at the service backend. IGM also flags WizardGain as dead in their tracking. Image wizardgain/worker still publishes but the upstream service is unreliable.
